summary: Comprehensive test suite (76 tests) covering all migration categories, cross-area flows, idempotent re-runs, and all 4 source types. Tests use temp directories properly via HOME env override and custom --target-workspace/--target-env flags. CLI integration via 'npm run migrate' in package.json. All 233 project tests pass. No blocking issues found.

issues.description: Several tests in 'CLI argument parsing', 'Skill conflict modes', 'Preset handling', and 'Include/Exclude filtering' sections are assertion-only tests that don't actually invoke the migration script. They test pure logic that could be validated through the integration tests. These aren't harmful but provide limited value compared to the full integration tests.

The 'existsSync' helper at the bottom of the file uses fs.access (async) in a synchronous context without await. It works because Node.js fs.access throws on error, but the import uses 'promises as fs' which returns a promise. The sync version should use require('fs').accessSync or the imported fs.accessSync. Currently the tests that call it use assert(existsSync(...)) which may not work correctly since it would return a Promise, not a boolean.

Tests use execSync for every migration invocation which means each test spawns a new tsx process. This is functional but slow (total suite takes ~24s). Consider grouping related tests in a single describe block that share the same source setup to reduce process spawns.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Migration tests use a specific pattern for temp isolation: mkdtemp() + HOME env override + --target-workspace/--target-env flags. This pattern should be documented for future test authors.
sharedStateObservations.detail: Migration test isolation pattern: use os.tmpdir() + fs.mkdtemp() for temp dirs, override HOME env to point temp home dirs, pass --target-workspace and --target-env to the script. This avoids touching real user data.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The migration-test command in services.yaml uses a separate bash script (tests/test-migration.sh) rather than the node test runner. The npm test command uses node --import tsx --test which runs all tests including migration tests.
sharedStateObservations.detail: services.yaml has a separate 'migration-test' command that uses tests/test-migration.sh. The main 'test' command runs all tests including migration tests via node --import tsx --test. Both approaches work but use different test infrastructure.
